file_insights.purpose: Establish consistent code style across editors and IDEs for Kotlin/Android development

patterns_discovered.description: Global settings ([*]) applied to all files, then overridden by language-specific sections
4-space indentation for code files (Kotlin, Gradle, XML), 2-space for data/config files (JSON, YAML)
Continuation indent size and 120-character line length limit aligned with ktlint standards
Proper use of wildcard patterns, boolean flags, and configuration syntax

gotchas_discovered.description: Markdown files explicitly exclude trim_trailing_whitespace to preserve formatting (common oversight)
Non-standard line length (typically 80/100) - should be documented if different from team guidelines
Uses string value 'off' rather than numeric - EditorConfig feature that not all editors support uniformly
Continuation indent explicitly set to 4 for Kotlin files - necessary for proper multi-line statement formatting

approach_outcome.description: EditorConfig file created successfully on first attempt with comprehensive Kotlin/Android-specific styling rules
approach_outcome.key_accomplishment: Single-pass implementation covering all major file types used in Kotlin/Android projects

recommendations.recommendation: Add EditorConfig plugin installation guidance to project onboarding documentation for IDEs that don't support it natively
Document the 120-character line length choice for Kotlin if it deviates from organizational standards
Consider adding configurations for .properties files and Gradle wrapper scripts (gradlew)
Add comments explaining why Markdown preserves trailing whitespace
Validate EditorConfig compatibility across team's primary IDEs/editors (VS Code, Android Studio, etc.)
